entities.name: danuglipron
entities.context: This patent is part of the chemical series from which danuglipron (PF-06882961) was developed by Pfizer. Danuglipron, derived from this chemical series, advanced to Phase 2/3 clinical trials.

entities.name: GLP-1 receptor
entities.context: These nonpeptide compounds activate the GLP-1 receptor through an allosteric mechanism, binding within a transmembrane pocket distinct from the orthosteric peptide-binding domain.

entities.name: DPP-4
entities.context: They are orally bioavailable and resistant to proteolytic degradation by DPP-4 and neutral endopeptidases.

entities.name: neutral endopeptidase
entities.context: They are orally bioavailable and resistant to proteolytic degradation by DPP-4 and neutral endopeptidases.

entities.name: small-molecule GLP-1 receptor agonism (allosteric)

attributes.binding_site: transmembrane allosteric pocket, distinct from orthosteric peptide-binding domain

entities.context: These nonpeptide compounds activate the GLP-1 receptor through an allosteric mechanism, binding within a transmembrane pocket distinct from the orthosteric peptide-binding domain.

entities.name: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus
entities.context: Therapeutic indications include type 2 diabetes mellitus, obesity, non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH), cardiovascular disease, and related metabolic disorders.

entities.name: Obesity
entities.context: Therapeutic indications include type 2 diabetes mellitus, obesity, non-alcoholic steatohepatitis (NASH), cardiovascular disease, and related metabolic disorders. Pfizer discontinued the twice-daily formulation but continues development of a once-daily extended-release formulation for obesity.

entities.name: Non-Alcoholic Steatohepatitis
